name: LLM Router
summary: Uses an LLM to classify the incoming payload and route it to one of the configured output ports.
description:
# LLM Router

Classifies the incoming payload using an LLM and routes it to a named output port based on the model's decision. Routes are defined in settings as a list of `{ name, description }` pairs. The node dynamically exposes one output port per route.

## Use cases
- Route support tickets to the correct team (billing, technical, general)
- Classify emails by intent (inquiry, complaint, order)
- Direct documents to different processing pipelines by type

## Notes
- Output ports are dynamic: the port names match the `name` field of each route in settings
- The LLM is given the route descriptions and instructed to pick the best match
- If no route matches confidently, the payload is emitted on the last-defined route as a fallback
- Combine with `flowdsl/llm-analyzer` for richer multi-step classification

inputs.name: AnyPayload
inputs.description: Accepts any JSON payload to be classified and routed.

outputs.name: dynamic
outputs.description: Output ports are generated at flow-definition time, one per route defined in the 'routes' setting. Port names match the 'name' field of each route entry.

prompt.title: Classification Prompt
prompt.description: Describe what the LLM should classify. The route descriptions are appended automatically.

x-ui.placeholder: Classify this customer message by its primary intent.

routes.title: Routes
routes.description: List of named routes. Each route becomes an output port. The LLM uses the description to decide which route fits.

name.description: Route name. Also the output port name. PascalCase recommended.

description.description: What kind of payload belongs on this route. Used as context for the LLM.
examples: Questions about invoices, payments, or subscriptions
